title: تأثير زيادة وقت اللعب بالفيديو على الأطفال الصغار
text_original: day. Also, 2- to 4-year- olds saw an increase in total video game play from 16 minutes to 21 minutes, and we found a large (24-minute) jump from 40 minutes to an hour and four minutes of video game play for 5- to 8-year-olds. This age difference is especially notable for smartphone or tablet gaming, where 2- to 4-year-olds play for about 14 minutes on a typical day, while 5- to 8-year-olds play for more than twice as long, with 34 minutes of daily game play on smartphones or tablets. Households with handheld video game systems (e.g., Nintendo Switch) also increased 18% since 2017, with 31% of homes owning a handheld video game system in 2017 and 49% owning one in 2024. © COMMON SENSE MEDIA. text_simplified: infants and young children are spending more time playing video games on smartphones or tablets, with a significant increase for older children. This trend is concerning as it could affect their development.
